description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, with a prominent chimney rising from one side. The exterior walls are a mix of white plaster and exposed timber framing, giving it a classic half-timbered appearance. Moss and weathering are visible on the walls and roof, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The structure is elevated on wooden stilts, with the lower portion open and the upper story overhanging slightly. Windows are small and square, with a single window visible on the front gable. The overall design evokes a medieval or Tudor-style urban dwelling, compact and functional for dense neighborhoods.
description: A weathered, half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, reddish-brown tiles, and visible moss, elevated on wooden stilts.
description_view_0: Front view shows the narrow gable end, a single small window, and the house elevated on stilts.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the long expanse of the roof, extensive timber framing, and mossy plaster walls.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a plain gable end with no windows and a continuation of the timber and plaster pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the other side, with visible chimney and timber framing details.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door, elevated entry with no visible porch, accessed via steps or platform, minimal decorative elements.
